assumptions_or_open_questions: The two eprintln! blocks (lines 72-77 and 92-97) and the --debug-file argument setup (lines 432-449) in src/adapters/process_backend.rs are not present on master and were added as debugging scaffolding during development of this branch — removing them restores the master-branch behavior for those code paths.
The result symlink at the repo root is a local Nix build artifact that should be git-ignored or unstaged; removing it from version control does not affect builds or CI.
No other unrelated changes remain on the branch beyond these three items — the artifact-metadata fix itself requires no further modification.
data.problem_framing: The core artifact-metadata fix is fully approved: all four source files (service.rs, completion.rs, prompt_review.rs, final_review.rs) correctly source RecordProducer from InvocationEnvelope.metadata, cargo check passes, both targeted regression tests pass, and the full 86-test lib suite passes. Three pending amendments remain from the final review — all are cleanup of unrelated changes that were included on the branch: (1) two eprintln! debug-logging blocks in process_backend.rs lines 72-77 and 92-97, (2) unconditional --debug-file argument construction at lines 432-449 in process_backend.rs, and (3) the result symlink which is a local Nix build artifact. These must be removed to keep the branch cleanly scoped to the artifact-metadata fix.

proposed_work.details: Remove the two eprintln! blocks at lines 72-77 and 92-97 in src/adapters/process_backend.rs. These unconditionally dump stdout/stderr content and result payloads to stderr on every JSON parse failure. They are leftover debugging scaffolding unrelated to the artifact-metadata fix. The surrounding error-handling control flow (the Err match arms) should be preserved — only the eprintln! macro invocations and their arguments are removed.
proposed_work.summary: Remove eprintln! debug-logging blocks from process_backend.rs

proposed_work.details: Remove the debug_file path construction at lines 432-435 and the two argument pushes ("--debug-file" and the path) at lines 448-449 in src/adapters/process_backend.rs. This code unconditionally writes a debug log to runtime/temp/{invocation_id}.claude-debug.log for every Claude invocation, which is inappropriate for production. Removing it restores the master-branch argument list for the Claude CLI subprocess.

Run git rm --cached result to unstage the result symlink (a local Nix build artifact pointing to a nix store path). Optionally add 'result' to .gitignore if not already present, to prevent future accidental commits of local build artifacts.
proposed_work.summary: Remove result symlink from version control

proposed_work.details: After all three removals, run nix develop -c cargo check and nix develop -c cargo test --lib to confirm the branch still compiles cleanly and all 86 tests pass. This validates that the cleanup did not inadvertently break anything.
proposed_work.summary: Run cargo check and cargo test to validate cleanup

risks: The cleanup items are straightforward removals of additive debugging code and a build artifact — risk of introducing regressions is minimal, and will be caught by the post-cleanup cargo check and test run.
